Arnold I (died April 16, 1106), Count of Chiny, son of Louis II, Count of Chiny, and his wife Sophie. He succeeded his father as count before 1066.

Arnold is best known for his many clashes with the …

zh.Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Franc: 法國哈帕格-亞登奧布拉莫盧普特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Arnoul Ier de Chiny於從1035年到1045年出生在比利時盧布格尼時，他的父親Louis II de Chiny36歲，母親Sophie26歲。他於1058年與Adélaïs de Ramerupt在比利時盧布格尼結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1106年4月16日在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Franc: 法国哈帕格-亞登奧布拉莫盧普特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Arnoul Ier de Chiny于从1035年到1045年出生在比利时盧布格尼时，他的父亲Louis II de Chiny36岁，母亲Sophie26岁。他于1058年与Adélaïs de Ramerupt在比利时盧布格尼结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1106年4月16日在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chiny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Old English cin ‘chin’, as a nickname for someone with a prominent chin or else for a clean-shaven man.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 陳 (see   Chen   1) and 錢 (see   Qian   1).

variants.name: Chin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Wai, Hong, Fook, Chun, Lun, Ying, Young, Yuk, Kin, Leung, Mee.
English (Cornwall): variant of   Chinn  .
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surnames 金, 靳 and 晉, see   Jin   1-3.

variants.name: Ching
meanings: Cornish: dialect form of   Chinn  .
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surnames 荊, 景, 井, 敬, 靖 and 經, see   Jing   1-6.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 程, see   Cheng   1.

variants.name: Chen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Wei, Jian, Ming, Hong, Li, Yan, Wen, Jing, Ping, Ying, Feng, Mei.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 陳, meaning ‘to explain’ and ‘old’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Chen (陳), the name of a state (located mainly in present-day Henan and Anhui provinces) granted to Hu Gong Man (Duke Hu of Chen, reigned 1045–986  BC ). His descendants acquired 陳, the name of the state, as their surname. (ii) from the surname Hou Mo Chen (侯莫陳), borne by people from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. They adopted the surname 陳 after they moved to Luoyang (in present-day Henan province) during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Arnoul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Patrice, Pierre.
French: variant of   Arnaud  .

variants.name: Arnaud
meanings: Germanic. See Arnold .

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Arnoul Ier de Chiny was born from 1035 to 1045, in Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgium, his father, Louis II de Chiny, was 36 and his mother, Sophie, was 26. He married Adélaïs de Ramerupt in 1058, in Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgium. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 16 April 1106, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Arnoul Ier de Chiny est né du 1035 au 1045, à Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgique, son père, Louis II de Chiny, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Sophie, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Adélaïs de Ramerupt en 1058, à Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 16 avril 1106, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Arnoul Ier de Chiny von 1035 bis 1045 in Chiny, Luxemburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Louis II de Chiny 36 und seine Mutter Sophie 26. Er hat 1058 in Chiny, Luxemburg, Belgien Adélaïs de Ramerupt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 16. April 1106 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Arnoul Ier de Chiny è nato il da 1035 a 1045 nel Chiny, Lussemburgo, Belgio, il suo padre, Louis II de Chiny, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Sophie, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Adélaïs de Ramerupt nel 1058, nel Chiny, Lussemburgo, Belgio. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 aprile 1106, nel sua cittadina.

ja.Chiny, Lotharingia, France: ベルギールクセンボウルグチニー
ja.Circa 1068 Chiny, Walloon, Luxembourg,: ベルギールクセンボウルグチニー
ja.Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgium: ベルギールクセンボウルグチニー
ja.Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Franc: フランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアウベラムルプト
ja.Chiny, Lotharingia, Holy Roman Empire: ベルギールクセンボウルグチニー
ja.lifeSummary: Arnoul Ier de Chinyがから1035年まで1045年にベルギールクセンボウルグチニーで生まれた時，父Louis II de Chinyは36歳，母Sophieは26歳であった。1058年にベルギールクセンボウルグチニーでAdélaïs de Rameruptと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1106年4月16日に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnoul Ier de Chiny(이)가 1035년 부터 1045년 까지 벨기에 루세보우르그 친이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Louis II de Chiny(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophie(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1058년 벨기에 루세보우르그 친이에서 Adélaïs de Ramerupt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1106년 4월 16일 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

lv.from 1035 to 1045–16 April 1106: no 1035. gada līdz 1045. gadam–1106. gada 16. aprīlis

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Arnoul Ier de Chiny nasceu de 1035 a 1045, em Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Louis II de Chiny, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Sophie, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Adélaïs de Ramerupt em 1058, em Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 16 de abril de 1106, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Arnoul Ier de Chiny родился с 1035 по 1045 в местности чини, луксембург, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Louis II de Chiny было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Sophie, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Adélaïs de Ramerupt в 1058 г. в местности чини, луксембург, Бельгия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 16 апреля 1106 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Arnoul Ier de Chiny nació desde 1035 hasta 1045, en Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Louis II de Chiny, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Sophie, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Adélaïs de Ramerupt en 1058, en Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de abril de 1106, en su pueblo.
